items.name: TM Light That Burns the Sky

species.name: Abomasnow
species.pokedex_entry: A powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.

species.name: Abra
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon that sleeps 18 hours a day. Observation revealed that it uses Teleport to change its location once every hour.

species.name: Absol
species.pokedex_entry: It sharply senses even subtle changes in the sky and the land to predict natural disasters. It is a long-lived Pokémon that has a life-span of 100 years.

species.name: Accelgor
species.pokedex_entry: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.

species.name: Aegislash
species.pokedex_entry: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

species.name: Aerodactyl
species.pokedex_entry: Aerodactyl is a Pokémon from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from DNA extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies.

species.name: Aggron
species.pokedex_entry: Its iron horns grow longer a little at a time. They are used to determine the Aggron's age. The gouges in its armor are worn with pride as mementos from battles.

species.name: Aipom
species.pokedex_entry: Its tail ends with a dexterous, handlike appendage. However, because it uses the tail so much, Aipom's real hands have become rather clumsy.

species.name: Alakazam
species.pokedex_entry: While it has strong psychic abilities and high intelligence, an Alakazam's muscles are very weak. It uses psychic power to move its body.

species.name: Alcremie
species.pokedex_entry: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

species.name: Alomomola
species.pokedex_entry: The reason it helps Pokémon in a weakened condition is that any Pokémon coming after them may also attack Alomomola.

species.name: Altaria
species.pokedex_entry: It hums in a beautiful soprano voice. It flies among white clouds in the blue sky. It launches intensely hot fireballs from its mouth.

species.name: Amaura
species.pokedex_entry: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

species.name: Ambipom
species.pokedex_entry: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

species.name: Amoonguss
species.pokedex_entry: Amoonguss generally doesn’t move much. It tends to stand still near Poké Balls that have been dropped on the ground.

species.name: Ampharos
species.pokedex_entry: It gives off so much light that it can be seen even from space. People in the old days used its light to send signals back and forth with others far away.

species.name: Annihilape
species.pokedex_entry: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

species.name: Anorith
species.pokedex_entry: It was resurrected from a fossil using the power of science. It swims by undulating the eight wings at its sides. They were feet that adapted to life in the sea.

species.name: Appletun
species.pokedex_entry: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

species.name: Applin
species.pokedex_entry: As soon as it’s born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

species.name: Araquanid
species.pokedex_entry: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.

species.name: Arbok
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon has a terrifically strong constricting power. It can even flatten steel oil drums. Once it wraps its body around its foe, escaping is impossible.

species.name: Arboliva
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon drives back enemies by launching its rich, aromatic oil at them with enough force to smash a boulder.

species.name: Arcanine
species.pokedex_entry: This fleet-footed Pokémon is said to run over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within its body is its source of power.

species.name: Arceus
species.pokedex_entry: It is described in mythology as the Pokémon that shaped the universe with its 1,000 arms.

species.name: Archaludon
species.pokedex_entry: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

species.name: Archen
species.pokedex_entry: Archen is said to be the ancestor of bird Pokémon. It lived in treetops, eating berries and bug Pokémon.

species.name: Archeops
species.pokedex_entry: Though capable of flight, Archeops was apparently better at hunting on the ground.

species.name: Arctibax
species.pokedex_entry: It attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax’s strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.

species.name: Arctovish
species.pokedex_entry: The skin on its face is impervious to attack, but breathing difficulties made this Pokémon go extinct anyway.

species.name: Arctozolt
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon lived on prehistoric seashores and was able to preserve food with the ice on its body. It went extinct because it moved so slowly.

species.name: Ariados
species.pokedex_entry: Its feet are tipped with tiny hooked claws that enable it to scuttle on ceilings and vertical walls. It constricts its foe with thin and strong silk webbing.

species.name: Armaldo
species.pokedex_entry: Armaldo usually lives on land. However, when it hunts for prey, it dives beneath the ocean. It swims around using its two large wings.

species.name: Armarouge
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.

species.name: Aromatisse
species.pokedex_entry: The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight.

species.name: Aron
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon that is clad in steel armor. A new suit of armor is made when it evolves. The old, discarded armor is salvaged as metal for making iron products.

species.name: Arrokuda
species.pokedex_entry: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.

species.name: Articuno
species.pokedex_entry: Articuno is a legendary bird Pokémon that can control ice. The flapping of its wings chills the air. As a result, it is said that when this Pokémon flies, snow will fall.

species.name: Audino
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.

species.name: Aurorus
species.pokedex_entry: Using its diamond-shaped crystals, it can instantly create a wall of ice to block an opponent's attack.

species.name: Avalugg
species.pokedex_entry: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.

species.name: Axew
species.pokedex_entry: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

species.name: Azelf
species.pokedex_entry: The dreaded Being of Willpower. Legends tell of this Pokémon manipulating the will of its adversaries and turning them into puppets of its own.

species.name: Azumarill
species.pokedex_entry: It lives in water virtually all day long. Its body color and pattern act as camouflage that makes it tough for enemies to spot in water.

species.name: Azurill
species.pokedex_entry: Its tail, which is packed with nutrition, is very bouncy like a rubber ball. On sunny days they gather at the edge of water and splash about for fun.

species.name: Bagon
species.pokedex_entry: Although it is small, this Pokémon is very powerful because its body is a bundle of muscles. It launches head-butts with its ironlike skull.

species.name: Baltoy
species.pokedex_entry: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

species.name: Banette
species.pokedex_entry: An abandoned plush doll became this Pokémon. They are said to live in garbage dumps and wander about in search of the children that threw them away.

species.name: Barbaracle
species.pokedex_entry: Having an eye on each palm allows it to keep watch in all directions. In a pinch, its limbs start to act on their own to ensure the enemy’s defeat.

species.name: Barboach
species.pokedex_entry: Makes its home in swamps with murky water. The poor visibility hides this Pokémon from predators, and the slime on its body makes grasping it difficult.

species.name: Barraskewda
species.pokedex_entry: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

species.name: Basculegion
species.pokedex_entry: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

species.name: Basculin
species.pokedex_entry: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

species.name: Bastiodon
species.pokedex_entry: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

species.name: Baxcalibur
species.pokedex_entry: It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

species.name: Bayleef
species.pokedex_entry: A Bayleef's neck is ringed by curled-up leaves. Inside each leaf is a small tree shoot. The fragrance of this shoot makes people peppy.

species.name: Beartic
species.pokedex_entry: It swims energetically through frigid seas. When it gets tired, it freezes the seawater with its breath so it can rest on the ice.

species.name: Beautifly
species.pokedex_entry: A colorful and incredibly beautiful but also greedy Pokémon. In an effort to keep its favorite food all to itself, it will chase away Combee as they try to gather nectar.

species.name: Beedrill
species.pokedex_entry: A Beedrill is extremely territorial. For safety reasons, no one should ever approach its nest. If angered, they will attack in a swarm.

species.name: Beheeyem
species.pokedex_entry: Sometimes found drifting above wheat fields, this Pokémon can control the memories of its opponents.

species.name: Beldum
species.pokedex_entry: When Beldum gather in a swarm, they move in perfect unison as if they were but one Pokémon. They communicate with each other using brain waves.

species.name: Bellibolt
species.pokedex_entry: What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.

species.name: Bellossom
species.pokedex_entry: Its flower petals deepen in color through exposure to sunlight. When cloudy weather persists, it does a dance that is thought to be a ritual for summoning the sun.

species.name: Bellsprout
species.pokedex_entry: A Bellsprout's thin and flexible body lets it bend and sway to avoid any attack, however strong it may be. From its mouth, it leaks a fluid that melts even iron.

species.name: Bergmite
species.pokedex_entry: Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.

species.name: Bewear
species.pokedex_entry: It waves its hands wildly in intimidation and warning. Life is over for anyone who doesn't run away as fast as possible.

species.name: Bibarel
species.pokedex_entry: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

species.name: Bidoof
species.pokedex_entry: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

species.name: Binacle
species.pokedex_entry: If the two don’t work well together, both their offense and defense fall apart. Without good teamwork, they won’t survive.

species.name: Bisharp
species.pokedex_entry: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

species.name: Blacephalon
species.pokedex_entry: A UB that appeared from an Ultra Wormhole, it causes explosions, then takes advantage of opponents’ surprise to rob them of their vitality.

species.name: Blastoise
species.pokedex_entry: The waterspouts that protrude from its shell are highly accurate. Their bullets of water can precisely nail tin cans from a distance of over 160 feet.

species.name: Blaziken
species.pokedex_entry: It learns martial arts that use punches and kicks. Every several years, its old feathers burn off, and new, supple feathers grow back in their place.

species.name: Blipbug
species.pokedex_entry: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

species.name: Blissey
species.pokedex_entry: If it senses sadness with its fluffy fur, a Blissey will rush over to the sad person, however far away, to share an egg of happiness that brings a smile to any face.

species.name: Blitzle
species.pokedex_entry: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

species.name: Boldore
species.pokedex_entry: It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.

species.name: Boltund
species.pokedex_entry: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

species.name: Bombirdier
species.pokedex_entry: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

species.name: Bonsly
species.pokedex_entry: It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body's fluid levels by eliminating excess.

species.name: Bouffalant
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason.

species.name: Bounsweet
species.pokedex_entry: When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.

species.name: Braixen
species.pokedex_entry: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

species.name: Brambleghast
species.pokedex_entry: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

species.name: Bramblin
species.pokedex_entry: Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

species.name: Braviary
species.pokedex_entry: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.

species.name: Breloom
species.pokedex_entry: It scatters spores from holes in the cap on its head. It loves warm and humid climates. It feeds on trees and plants in fields and forests.

species.name: Brionne
species.pokedex_entry: On nights when the sea is calm, Brionne dance with one another to the singing of the Primarina that’s leading them.

species.name: Bronzong
species.pokedex_entry: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.

species.name: Bronzor
species.pokedex_entry: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.

species.name: Brute Bonnet
species.pokedex_entry: It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.

species.name: Bruxish
species.pokedex_entry: When it unleashes its psychic power from the protuberance on its head, the grating sound of grinding teeth echoes through the area.

species.name: Budew
species.pokedex_entry: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.

species.name: Buizel
species.pokedex_entry: It moves freely in the water by spinning its forked tail for propulsion. The resemblance to the screw of a steamboat is coincidental.

species.name: Bulbasaur
species.pokedex_entry: Bulbasaur can be seen napping in bright sunlight. There is a seed on its back. By soaking up the sun's rays, the seed grows progressively larger.

species.name: Buneary
species.pokedex_entry: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

species.name: Bunnelby
species.pokedex_entry: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

species.name: Burmy
species.pokedex_entry: If its cloak is even slightly damaged, Burmy will immediately repair it with whatever is close at hand. The Pokémon within the cloak is scrawny and vulnerable to the cold.

species.name: Butterfree
species.pokedex_entry: It has a superior ability to search for delicious honey from flowers. It can seek, extract, and carry honey from flowers blooming over six miles away.

species.name: Buzzwole
species.pokedex_entry: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

species.name: Cacnea
species.pokedex_entry: Cacnea live in deserts with virtually no rainfall. It battles by swinging its thick, spiked arms. Once a year, a yellow flower blooms.

species.name: Cacturne
species.pokedex_entry: After spending thousands of years in harsh deserts, its blood transformed into the same substances as sand. It is nocturnal, so it hunts at night.

species.name: Calyrex
species.pokedex_entry: Calyrex is known in legend as a king that ruled over Galar in ancient times. It has the power to cause hearts to mend and plants to spring forth.

species.name: Camerupt
species.pokedex_entry: The humps on Camerupt’s back are formed by a transformation of its bones. They sometimes blast out molten magma. This Pokémon apparently erupts often when it is enraged.

species.name: Capsakid
species.pokedex_entry: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

species.name: Carbink
species.pokedex_entry: Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it defends itself by firing beams from the jewel part of its body.

species.name: Carkol
species.pokedex_entry: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Carnivine
species.pokedex_entry: Though this is a plant Pokémon, it has a gluttonous and unruly temperament. Carnivine attacks its prey with its cavernous maw wide open.

species.name: Carracosta
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon emerges from the water in search of prey despite the fact that it moves more slowly on land.

species.name: Carvanha
species.pokedex_entry: Carvanha attack ships in swarms, making them sink. Although it is said to be a very vicious Pokémon, it timidly flees as soon as it finds itself alone.

species.name: Cascoon
species.pokedex_entry: The silk coating its body is thin but sufficiently strong. Cascoon's silk has a luster and texture superior to that of Silcoon's, and clothes made using Cascoon silk are regarded as top-notch.

species.name: Castform
species.pokedex_entry: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

species.name: Castform
species.pokedex_entry: It alters its form depending on the weather. Changes in the climate such as the temperature and humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

species.name: Castform
species.pokedex_entry: It alters its form depending on the weather. Changes in the climate such as the temperature and humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

species.name: Castform
species.pokedex_entry: It alters its form depending on the weather. Changes in the climate such as the temperature and humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

species.name: Caterpie
species.pokedex_entry: Its voracious appetite compels it to devour leaves bigger than itself without hesitation. It releases a terribly strong odor from its antennae.

species.name: Celebi
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon came from the future by crossing over time. It is thought that so long as Celebi appears, a bright and shining future awaits us.

species.name: Celesteela
species.pokedex_entry: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

species.name: Centiskorch
species.pokedex_entry: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

species.name: Ceruledge
species.pokedex_entry: An old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.

species.name: Cetitan
species.pokedex_entry: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

species.name: Cetoddle
species.pokedex_entry: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

species.name: Chandelure
species.pokedex_entry: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

species.name: Chansey
species.pokedex_entry: Chansey lay nutritionally excellent eggs every day. The eggs are so delicious, they are eagerly devoured by even those who have lost their appetite.

species.name: Charcadet
species.pokedex_entry: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

species.name: Charizard
species.pokedex_entry: A Charizard flies about in search of strong opponents. It breathes intense flames that can melt any material. However, it will never torch a weaker foe.

species.name: Charjabug
species.pokedex_entry: Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.

species.name: Charmander
species.pokedex_entry: The flame that burns at the tip of its tail is an indication of its emotions. The flame wavers when Charmander is happy, and blazes when it is enraged.

species.name: Charmeleon
species.pokedex_entry: Without pity, its sharp claws destroy foes. If it encounters a strong enemy, it becomes agitated, and the flame on its tail flares with a bluish white color.

species.name: Chatot
species.pokedex_entry: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.

species.name: Cherrim
species.pokedex_entry: Motionless, save for the occasional quiver. A rich array of Pokémon can be found gathered around it, drawn by the scent exuded from Cherrim's folded petals.

species.name: Cherubi
species.pokedex_entry: Once the fruit growing alongside the main body is large and plump, Cherubi will use the nutrients within to evolve. The fruit then detaches, becoming nourishment for other creatures.

species.name: Chesnaught
species.pokedex_entry: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

species.name: Chespin
species.pokedex_entry: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

species.name: Chewtle
species.pokedex_entry: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

species.name: Chi-Yu
species.pokedex_entry: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

species.name: Chien-Pao
species.pokedex_entry: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

species.name: Chikorita
species.pokedex_entry: It waves its leaf around to keep foes at bay. However, a sweet fragrance also wafts from the leaf, creating a friendly atmosphere that becalms the battlers.

species.name: Chimchar
species.pokedex_entry: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

species.name: Chimecho
species.pokedex_entry: Can emit waves of air powerful enough to knock out prey taller than itself. I hypothesize that it amplifies the faint sound of wind within its body.

species.name: Chinchou
species.pokedex_entry: When it senses danger, it discharges positive and negative electricity from its two antennae. It lives in depths beyond sunlight's reach.

species.name: Chingling
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon gave me an excruciating headache when it seemingly cried out without making a sound. Perhaps there are some sounds that the human ear is simply incapable of hearing.

species.name: Cinccino
species.pokedex_entry: A special oil that seeps through their fur helps them avoid attacks. The oil fetches a high price at market.

species.name: Cinderace
species.pokedex_entry: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

species.name: Clamperl
species.pokedex_entry: Clamperl’s pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder’s pearls.

species.name: Clauncher
species.pokedex_entry: By detonating gas that accumulates in its right claw, this Pokémon launches water like a bullet. This is how Clauncher defeats its enemies.

species.name: Clawitzer
species.pokedex_entry: Clawitzer’s right arm is a cannon that launches projectiles made of seawater. Shots from a Clawitzer’s cannon arm can sink a tanker.

species.name: Claydol
species.pokedex_entry: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

species.name: Clefable
species.pokedex_entry: A Clefable uses its wings to skip lightly as if it were flying. Its bouncy step lets it even walk on water. On quiet, moonlit nights, it strolls on lakes.

species.name: Clefairy
species.pokedex_entry: On every night of a full moon, they come out to play. When dawn arrives, the tired Clefairy go to sleep nestled up against each other in deep and quiet mountains.

species.name: Cleffa
species.pokedex_entry: On nights with many shooting stars, Cleffa can be seen dancing in a ring. They dance until daybreak, when they quench their thirst with the morning dew.

species.name: Clobbopus
species.pokedex_entry: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

species.name: Clodsire
species.pokedex_entry: It lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.

species.name: Cloyster
species.pokedex_entry: It swims in the sea by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. The Cloyster shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.

species.name: Coalossal
species.pokedex_entry: While it’s engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

species.name: Cobalion
species.pokedex_entry: From the moment it’s born, this Pokémon radiates the air of a leader. Its presence will calm even vicious foes.

species.name: Cofagrigus
species.pokedex_entry: They pretend to be elaborate coffins to teach lessons to grave robbers. Their bodies are covered in pure gold.

species.name: Combee
species.pokedex_entry: They swear fealty to a queen Pokémon and work diligently to gather nectar. Each swarm produces a different flavor of honey.

species.name: Combusken
species.pokedex_entry: It lashes out with 10 kicks per second. Its strong fighting instinct compels it to keep up its offensive until the opponent gives up.

species.name: Comfey
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon smell very nice. All Comfey wear different flowers, so each of these Pokémon has its own individual scent.

species.name: Conkeldurr
species.pokedex_entry: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.

species.name: Copperajah
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

species.name: Corphish
species.pokedex_entry: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.

species.name: Corsola
species.pokedex_entry: Corsola live in warm southern seas. If the sea becomes polluted, the beautiful coral stalks become discolored and crumble away in tatters.

species.name: Corviknight
species.pokedex_entry: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region’s airborne taxi service.

species.name: Corvisquire
species.pokedex_entry: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent’s strength.

species.name: Cosmoem
species.pokedex_entry: It sucks in dust from the air at an astounding rate, frantically building up energy within its core as preparation for evolution.

species.name: Cosmog
species.pokedex_entry: Cosmog is very curious but not very cautious, often placing itself in danger. If things start to look dicey, it teleports away.

species.name: Cottonee
species.pokedex_entry: They go wherever the wind takes them. On rainy days, their bodies are heavier, so they take shelter beneath big trees.

species.name: Crabominable
species.pokedex_entry: Before it stops to think, it just starts pummeling. There are records of its turning back avalanches with a flurry of punches.

species.name: Crabrawler
species.pokedex_entry: Crabrawler has been known to mistake Exeggutor for a coconut tree and climb it. The enraged Exeggutor shakes it off and stomps it.

species.name: Cradily
species.pokedex_entry: It drags its heavy body along the seafloor. It makes its nest in the shallows of warm seas. Cradily can be seen on beaches when the tide goes out.

species.name: Cramorant
species.pokedex_entry: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.

species.name: Cranidos
species.pokedex_entry: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

species.name: Crawdaunt
species.pokedex_entry: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.

species.name: Cresselia
species.pokedex_entry: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

species.name: Croagunk
species.pokedex_entry: A poison wielder with a dastardly personality. Despite such qualities, this species is afforded a measure of popularity due to its peculiar cry and comical features.

species.name: Crobat
species.pokedex_entry: Over the course of evolution, its hind legs turned into wings. By alternately resting its front and rear wings, it can fly all day without having to stop.

species.name: Crocalor
species.pokedex_entry: The valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

species.name: Croconaw
species.pokedex_entry: Once its jaws clamp down on its foe, it will absolutely not let go. Because the tips of its fangs are forked back like fishhooks, they become irremovably embedded.

species.name: Crustle
species.pokedex_entry: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They’re mighty enough to crack Rhyperior’s carapace.

species.name: Cryogonal
species.pokedex_entry: When the weather gets hot, these Pokémon turn into water vapor. Cryogonal are almost never seen during summer.

species.name: Cubchoo
species.pokedex_entry: It sniffles before performing a move, using its frosty snot to provide an icy element to any move that needs it.

species.name: Cubone
species.pokedex_entry: It pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull it wears are from its tears.

species.name: Cufant
species.pokedex_entry: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

species.name: Cursola
species.pokedex_entry: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You’ll become stiff as stone if you touch it.

species.name: Cutiefly
species.pokedex_entry: An opponent’s aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent’s next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

species.name: Cyclizar
species.pokedex_entry: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.

species.name: Cyndaquil
species.pokedex_entry: It flares flames from its back to protect itself. The fire burns vigorously if the Pokémon is angry. When it is tired, it sputters with incomplete combustion.

species.name: Dachsbun
species.pokedex_entry: The surface of this Pokémon’s skin hardens when exposed to intense heat, and its body has an appetizing aroma.

species.name: Darkrai
species.pokedex_entry: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

species.name: Darmanitan
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Dartrix
species.pokedex_entry: Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.

species.name: Darumaka
species.pokedex_entry: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

species.name: Decidueye
species.pokedex_entry: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

species.name: Dedenne
species.pokedex_entry: Since Dedenne can’t generate much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

species.name: Deerling
species.pokedex_entry: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

species.name: Deino
species.pokedex_entry: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

species.name: Delcatty
species.pokedex_entry: Delcatty sleeps anywhere it wants without keeping a permanent nest. If other Pokémon approach it as it sleeps, this Pokémon will never fight—it will just move away somewhere else.

species.name: Delibird
species.pokedex_entry: It carries food bundled up in its tail. There was a famous explorer who managed to scale Mt. Everest thanks to a Delibird sharing its food.

species.name: Delphox
species.pokedex_entry: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

species.name: Deoxys
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon that mutated from an extraterrestrial virus exposed to a laser beam. Its body is configured for superior agility and speed.

species.name: Dewgong
species.pokedex_entry: It loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this Pokémon sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.

species.name: Dewott
species.pokedex_entry: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.

species.name: Dewpider
species.pokedex_entry: Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.

species.name: Dhelmise
species.pokedex_entry: The soul of seaweed adrift in the waves became reborn as this Pokémon. It maintains itself with new infusions of seabed detritus and seaweed.

species.name: Dialga
species.pokedex_entry: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

species.name: Diancie
species.pokedex_entry: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

species.name: Diggersby
species.pokedex_entry: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

species.name: Diglett
species.pokedex_entry: Diglett are raised in most farms. The reason is simple--wherever they burrow, the soil is left perfectly tilled for growing delicious crops.

species.name: Dipplin
species.pokedex_entry: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

species.name: Ditto
species.pokedex_entry: A Ditto rearranges its cell structure to transform itself. However, if it tries to change based on its memory, it will get details wrong.

species.name: Dodrio
species.pokedex_entry: A peculiar Pokémon species with three heads. It vigorously races across grassy plains even in arid seasons with little rainfall.

species.name: Doduo
species.pokedex_entry: Even while eating or sleeping, one of the heads remains always vigilant for any sign of danger. When threatened, it flees at over 60 miles per hour.

species.name: Dolliv
species.pokedex_entry: It basks in the sun to its heart’s content until the fruits on its head ripen. After that, Dolliv departs from human settlements and goes on a journey.

species.name: Dondozo
species.pokedex_entry: It treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.

species.name: Donphan
species.pokedex_entry: A Donphan is so strong it can easily haul a dump truck. Its hide has toughened to a rock-hard state. An ordinary sort of attack won't even leave a scratch.

species.name: Dottler
species.pokedex_entry: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

species.name: Doublade
species.pokedex_entry: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

species.name: Dracovish
species.pokedex_entry: Its mighty legs are capable of running at speeds exceeding 40 mph, but this Pokémon can’t breathe unless it’s underwater.

species.name: Dracozolt
species.pokedex_entry: The powerful muscles in its tail generate its electricity. Compared to its lower body, its upper half is entirely too small.

species.name: Dragalge
species.pokedex_entry: Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean’s surface and bathing it in sunlight.

species.name: Dragapult
species.pokedex_entry: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult’s horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

species.name: Dragonair
species.pokedex_entry: A Dragonair stores an enormous amount of energy inside its body. It is said to alter the weather around it by loosing energy from the crystals on its neck and tail.

species.name: Dragonite
species.pokedex_entry: It can circle the globe in just 16 hours. It is a kindhearted Pokémon that leads lost and foundering ships in a storm to the safety of land.

species.name: Drakloak
species.pokedex_entry: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it’ll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

species.name: Drampa
species.pokedex_entry: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

species.name: Drapion
species.pokedex_entry: Has a brutish, ferocious temperament. With immense strength and a sturdy shell off which swords will bounce, it rampages about and wreaks havoc.

species.name: Dratini
species.pokedex_entry: A Dratini continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.

species.name: Drednaw
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

species.name: Dreepy
species.pokedex_entry: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

species.name: Drifblim
species.pokedex_entry: It's drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

species.name: Drifloon
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

species.name: Drilbur
species.pokedex_entry: It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

species.name: Drizzile
species.pokedex_entry: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.

species.name: Drowzee
species.pokedex_entry: If your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it's a sure sign that a Drowzee is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.

species.name: Druddigon
species.pokedex_entry: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

species.name: Dubwool
species.pokedex_entry: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

species.name: Ducklett
species.pokedex_entry: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

species.name: Dudunsparce
species.pokedex_entry: It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

species.name: Dugtrio
species.pokedex_entry: Because the triplets originally split from one body, they think exactly alike. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly through the ground.

species.name: Dunsparce
species.pokedex_entry: Its drill-tipped tail is used to burrow into the ground backwards. This Pokémon is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.

species.name: Duosion
species.pokedex_entry: When their brains, now divided in two, are thinking the same thoughts, these Pokémon exhibit their maximum power.

species.name: Duraludon
species.pokedex_entry: The special metal that composes its body is very light, so this Pokémon has considerable agility. It lives in caves because it dislikes the rain.

species.name: Durant
species.pokedex_entry: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

species.name: Dusclops
species.pokedex_entry: It is thought that its body is hollow with only a spectral ball of fire burning inside. However, no one has been able to confirm this theory as fact.

species.name: Dusknoir
species.pokedex_entry: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

species.name: Duskull
species.pokedex_entry: A glare from its single scarlet eye makes even burly grown-ups freeze in utter fear. It is a nocturnal Pokémon that roams about under the cloak of darkness.

species.name: Dustox
species.pokedex_entry: Tends to be drawn to bonfires on dark nights. Difficult to chase away from settlements because of the way it scatters highly toxic scales.

species.name: Dwebble
species.pokedex_entry: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

species.name: Eelektrik
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

species.name: Eelektross
species.pokedex_entry: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

species.name: Eevee
species.pokedex_entry: An Eevee has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various stones causes this Pokémon to evolve.

species.name: Eiscue
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

species.name: Ekans
species.pokedex_entry: An Ekans curls itself up in a spiral while it rests. This position allows it to quickly respond to an enemy from any direction with a threat from its upraised head.

species.name: Eldegoss
species.pokedex_entry: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

species.name: Electabuzz
species.pokedex_entry: When a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.

species.name: Electivire
species.pokedex_entry: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

species.name: Electrike
species.pokedex_entry: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

species.name: Electrode
species.pokedex_entry: They appear in great numbers at electric power plants. Because they feed on electricity, they cause massive and chaotic blackouts in nearby cities.

species.name: Elekid
species.pokedex_entry: If it touches metal and discharges the electricity it has stored in its body, an Elekid begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.

species.name: Elgyem
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon was discovered about 50 years ago. Its highly developed brain enables it to exert its psychic powers.

species.name: Emboar
species.pokedex_entry: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

species.name: Emolga
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.

species.name: Empoleon
species.pokedex_entry: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

species.name: Enamorus
species.pokedex_entry: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

species.name: Entei
species.pokedex_entry: Entei embodies the passion of magma. It is thought to have been born in the eruption of a volcano. It blasts fire that consumes all that it touches.

species.name: Escavalier
species.pokedex_entry: It charges its enemies, lances at the ready. An image of one of its duels is captured in a famous painting of Escavalier clashing with Sirfetch’d.

species.name: Espathra
species.pokedex_entry: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

species.name: Espeon
species.pokedex_entry: An Espeon is extremely loyal to any trainer it considers to be worthy. It is said to have developed precognitive powers to protect its trainer from harm.

species.name: Espurr
species.pokedex_entry: There’s enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can’t be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.

species.name: Eternatus
species.pokedex_entry: It was inside a meteorite that fell 20,000 years ago. There seems to be a connection between this Pokémon and the Dynamax phenomenon.

species.name: Excadrill
species.pokedex_entry: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

species.name: Exeggcute
species.pokedex_entry: It consists of six eggs that care for each other. The eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear, it is close to evolution.

species.name: Exeggutor
species.pokedex_entry: Originally from the tropics, Exeggutor's heads grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall, they group to form an Exeggcute.

species.name: Exploud
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon can do more than just shout. To communicate with others of its kind, it’ll emit all sorts of sounds from the holes in its body.

species.name: Falinks
species.pokedex_entry: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

species.name: Farfetch'd
species.pokedex_entry: It is always seen with a stick from a plant. Apparently, there are good sticks and bad sticks. This Pokémon occasionally fights with others over choice sticks.

species.name: Farigiraf
species.pokedex_entry: The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.

species.name: Fearow
species.pokedex_entry: Its long neck and elongated beak are ideal for catching prey in soil or water. It deftly moves this extended and skinny beak to pluck prey.

species.name: Feebas
species.pokedex_entry: Feebas live in ponds that are heavily infested with weeds. Because of its hopelessly shabby appearance, it seems as if few trainers raise it.

species.name: Fennekin
species.pokedex_entry: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

species.name: Feraligatr
species.pokedex_entry: It opens its huge mouth to intimidate enemies. In battle, it runs using its thick and powerful hind legs to charge the foe with incredible speed.

species.name: Ferroseed
species.pokedex_entry: They stick their spikes into cave walls and absorb the minerals they find in the rock.

species.name: Ferrothorn
species.pokedex_entry: They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them.

species.name: Fezandipiti
species.pokedex_entry: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

species.name: Fidough
species.pokedex_entry: The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.

species.name: Finizen
species.pokedex_entry: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

species.name: Finneon
species.pokedex_entry: What a gorgeous sight this Pokémon is as it swims with its long, pink-painted caudal fins fluttering behind it. Finneon's beautiful appearance has led to its nickname: ”finery fish.”

species.name: Flaaffy
species.pokedex_entry: Its fleece quality changes to generate strong static electricity with a small amount of wool. The bare, slick parts of its hide are shielded against electricity.

species.name: Flabébé
species.pokedex_entry: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it’s collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

species.name: Flamigo
species.pokedex_entry: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

species.name: Flapple
species.pokedex_entry: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

species.name: Flareon
species.pokedex_entry: Flareon's fluffy fur releases heat into the air so that its body does not get excessively hot. Its body temperature can rise to a maximum of 1,650 degrees F.

species.name: Fletchinder
species.pokedex_entry: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

species.name: Fletchling
species.pokedex_entry: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

species.name: Flittle
species.pokedex_entry: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

species.name: Floatzel
species.pokedex_entry: Has a long, rather splendid flotation sac, which prevents Floatzel from drowning even in stormy seas. One might glimpse this species around fishing hamlets from time to time.

species.name: Floette
species.pokedex_entry: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

species.name: Floragato
species.pokedex_entry: The hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.

species.name: Florges
species.pokedex_entry: Its life can span several hundred years. It’s said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

species.name: Flutter Mane
species.pokedex_entry: It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.

species.name: Flygon
species.pokedex_entry: The flapping of its wings sounds like singing. To prevent detection by enemies, it hides itself by flapping up a cloud of desert sand.

species.name: Fomantis
species.pokedex_entry: When the sun rises, Fomantis spreads its four leaves and bathes in the sunlight. The tip of its head has a pleasant aroma.

species.name: Foongus
species.pokedex_entry: The spores released from this Pokémon’s hands are highly poisonous, but when thoroughly dried, the spores can be used as stomach medicine.

species.name: Forretress
species.pokedex_entry: It keeps itself inside its steel shell. The shell is opened when it is catching prey, but it is so quick that the shell's inside cannot be seen.

species.name: Fraxure
species.pokedex_entry: Their tusks can shatter rocks. Territory battles between Fraxure can be intensely violent.

species.name: Frigibax
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

species.name: Frillish
species.pokedex_entry: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

species.name: Froakie
species.pokedex_entry: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

species.name: Frogadier
species.pokedex_entry: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

species.name: Froslass
species.pokedex_entry: It freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees F. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.

species.name: Frosmoth
species.pokedex_entry: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

species.name: Fuecoco
species.pokedex_entry: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

species.name: Furfrou
species.pokedex_entry: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

species.name: Furret
species.pokedex_entry: A Furret has a very slim build. When under attack, it can squirm through narrow spaces and get away. In spite of its short limbs, it is very nimble and fleet.

species.name: Gabite
species.pokedex_entry: There is a long-held belief that medicine made from its scales will heal even incurable illnesses.

species.name: Gallade
species.pokedex_entry: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

species.name: Galvantula
species.pokedex_entry: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

species.name: Garbodor
species.pokedex_entry: They absorb garbage and make it part of their bodies. They shoot a poisonous liquid from their right-hand fingertips.

species.name: Garchomp
species.pokedex_entry: When it folds up its body and extends its wings, it looks like a jet plane. It flies at sonic speed.

species.name: Gardevoir
species.pokedex_entry: It apparently does not feel the pull of gravity because it supports itself with psychic power. It will give its life to protect its trainer.

species.name: Garganacl
species.pokedex_entry: Many Pokémon gather around Garganacl, hoping to lick at its mineral-rich salt.

species.name: Gastly
species.pokedex_entry: When exposed to a strong wind, a Gastly's gaseous body quickly dwindles away. They cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.

species.name: Gastrodon
species.pokedex_entry: Eats beach sand for nourishment. Should one Gastrodon encounter another of a different color, a fierce battle will inevitably ensue.

species.name: Genesect
species.pokedex_entry: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

species.name: Gengar
species.pokedex_entry: Deep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a Gengar running past you, pretending to be your shadow.

species.name: Geodude
species.pokedex_entry: It climbs mountain paths using only the power of its arms. Because they look just like boulders lining paths, hikers may step on them without noticing.

species.name: Gholdengo
species.pokedex_entry: It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.

species.name: Gible
species.pokedex_entry: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

species.name: Gigalith
species.pokedex_entry: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

species.name: Gimmighoul
species.pokedex_entry: It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

species.name: Girafarig
species.pokedex_entry: A Girafarig is an herbivore--it eats grass and tree shoots. While it is eating, its tail makes chewing and swallowing motions as if it were also eating.

species.name: Giratina
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.

species.name: Glaceon
species.pokedex_entry: As a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.

species.name: Glalie
species.pokedex_entry: A Glalie has the power to instantaneously freeze moisture in the atmosphere. A dazzling cloud of diamondlike ice crystals forms around its body.

species.name: Glameow
species.pokedex_entry: Bewitches humans with its helical tail and piercing gaze. Its hidden claws are quite sharp as well, making this Pokémon an exceedingly tricky opponent if antagonized.

species.name: Glastrier
species.pokedex_entry: Glastrier has tremendous physical strength, and the mask of ice covering its face is 100 times harder than diamond.

species.name: Gligar
species.pokedex_entry: It glides without making a single sound. It grasps the face of its foe using its hind and large front claws, then stabs with its poison barb.

species.name: Glimmet
species.pokedex_entry: Glimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.

species.name: Glimmora
species.pokedex_entry: Glimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.

species.name: Gliscor
species.pokedex_entry: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

species.name: Gloom
species.pokedex_entry: A horribly noxious honey drools from its mouth. One whiff of the honey can result in memory loss. Some fans are said to enjoy this overwhelming stink, however.

species.name: Gogoat
species.pokedex_entry: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

species.name: Golbat
species.pokedex_entry: Its fangs easily puncture even thick animal hide. It loves to feast on the blood of people and Pokémon. It flits about in darkness and strikes from behind.

species.name: Goldeen
species.pokedex_entry: In the springtime, schools of Goldeen can be seen swimming up falls and rivers. It metes out staggering damage with its single horn.

species.name: Golduck
species.pokedex_entry: A Golduck is an adept swimmer. It sometimes joins competitive swimmers in training. It uses psychic powers when its forehead shimmers with light.

species.name: Golem
species.pokedex_entry: It is said to live in volcanic craters on mountain peaks. Once a year, it sheds its hide and grows larger. The shed hide crumbles and returns to the soil.

species.name: Golett
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon are thought to have been created by the science of an ancient and mysterious civilization.

species.name: Golisopod
species.pokedex_entry: It battles skillfully with its six arms, but spends most of its time peacefully meditating in caves deep beneath the sea.

species.name: Golurk
species.pokedex_entry: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

species.name: Goodra
species.pokedex_entry: It gets picked on because it's meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.

species.name: Goomy
species.pokedex_entry: Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it needs some thorough hand-washing.

species.name: Gorebyss
species.pokedex_entry: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.

species.name: Gossifleur
species.pokedex_entry: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

species.name: Gothita
species.pokedex_entry: Even when nobody seems to be around, Gothita can still be heard making a muted cry. Many believe it’s speaking to something only it can see.

species.name: Gothitelle
species.pokedex_entry: A criminal who was shown his fate by a Gothitelle went missing that same day and was never seen again.

species.name: Gothorita
species.pokedex_entry: On nights when the stars shine, this Pokémon’s psychic power is at its strongest. It’s unknown just what link Gothorita has to the greater universe.

species.name: Gouging Fire
species.pokedex_entry: It resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.

species.name: Gourgeist
species.pokedex_entry: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

species.name: Grafaiai
species.pokedex_entry: Each Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.

species.name: Granbull
species.pokedex_entry: It has a particularly well-developed lower jaw. The huge fangs are heavy, causing it to tilt its head. Unless it is startled, it will not try to bite.

species.name: Grapploct
species.pokedex_entry: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

species.name: Graveler
species.pokedex_entry: They descend from mountains by tumbling down steep slopes. They are so brutal, they smash aside obstructing trees and massive boulders with thunderous tackles.

species.name: Great Tusk
species.pokedex_entry: This creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.

species.name: Greavard
species.pokedex_entry: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

species.name: Greedent
species.pokedex_entry: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.

species.name: Greninja
species.pokedex_entry: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

species.name: Grimer
species.pokedex_entry: Born from polluted sludge in the sea, Grimer's favorite food is anything filthy. They feed on wastewater pumped out from factories.

species.name: Grimmsnarl
species.pokedex_entry: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

species.name: Grookey
species.pokedex_entry: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

species.name: Grotle
species.pokedex_entry: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

species.name: Groudon
species.pokedex_entry: Groudon has appeared in mythology as the creator of the land. It sleeps in magma underground and is said to make volcanoes erupt on awakening.

species.name: Grovyle
species.pokedex_entry: Leaves grow out of this Pokémon's body. They help obscure a Grovyle from the eyes of its enemies while it is in a thickly overgrown forest.

species.name: Growlithe
species.pokedex_entry: Its superb sense of smell ensures that this Pokémon won't forget any scent, no matter what. It uses its sense of smell to detect the emotions of others.

species.name: Grubbin
species.pokedex_entry: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

species.name: Grumpig
species.pokedex_entry: Grumpig uses the black pearls on its body to wield its fantastic powers. When it is doing so, it dances bizarrely. This Pokémon’s black pearls are valuable as works of art.

species.name: Gulpin
species.pokedex_entry: Most of Gulpin’s body is made up of its stomach—its heart and brain are very small in comparison. This Pokémon’s stomach contains special enzymes that dissolve anything.

species.name: Gumshoos
species.pokedex_entry: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!

species.name: Gurdurr
species.pokedex_entry: Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there’s skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.

species.name: Guzzlord
species.pokedex_entry: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.

species.name: Gyarados
species.pokedex_entry: It is an extremely vicious and violent Pokémon. When humans begin to fight, it will appear and burn everything to the ground with intensely hot flames.

species.name: Hakamo-o
species.pokedex_entry: It sheds and regrows its scales on a continuous basis. The scales become harder and sharper each time they're regrown.

species.name: Happiny
species.pokedex_entry: It loves round white things. It carries an egg-shaped rock in imitation of Chansey.

species.name: Hariyama
species.pokedex_entry: Hariyama that are big and fat aren’t necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

species.name: Hatenna
species.pokedex_entry: If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.

species.name: Hatterene
species.pokedex_entry: If you’re too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.

species.name: Hattrem
species.pokedex_entry: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.

species.name: Haunter
species.pokedex_entry: If a Haunter beckons you while it is floating in darkness, don't approach it. This Pokémon will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.

species.name: Hawlucha
species.pokedex_entry: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

species.name: Haxorus
species.pokedex_entry: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

species.name: Heatmor
species.pokedex_entry: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

species.name: Heatran
species.pokedex_entry: Stories tell of this Pokémon being birthed from the boiling magma within Mount Coronet. Its molten-steel body holds many mysteries.

species.name: Heliolisk
species.pokedex_entry: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

species.name: Helioptile
species.pokedex_entry: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

species.name: Heracross
species.pokedex_entry: They gather in forests seeking the sweet sap of trees. It is completely clad in a steel-hard shell. It is proud of its horn, which it uses to fling foes.

species.name: Herdier
species.pokedex_entry: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.

species.name: Hippopotas
species.pokedex_entry: Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.

species.name: Hippowdon
species.pokedex_entry: Short-tempered and easily moved to violence. It whips up whirlwinds of sand to crush its foes' spirits, then goes in for the attack.

species.name: Hitmonchan
species.pokedex_entry: A Hitmonchan is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who aimed to become the world champion. Having an indomitable spirit means that it will never give up.

species.name: Hitmonlee
species.pokedex_entry: Its legs freely stretch and contract. Using these springlike limbs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its tired legs.

species.name: Hitmontop
species.pokedex_entry: Its technique of kicking while spinning is a remarkable mix of both offense and defense. Hitmontop travel faster spinning than they do walking.

species.name: Ho-Oh
species.pokedex_entry: Its feathers--which glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light--are thought to bring joy. It is said to live at the foot of a rainbow.

species.name: Honchkrow
species.pokedex_entry: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous Murkrow in tow.

species.name: Honedge
species.pokedex_entry: Apparently this Pokemon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

species.name: Hoopa
species.pokedex_entry: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

species.name: Hoothoot
species.pokedex_entry: It has an internal organ that senses the earth's rotation. Using this special organ, a Hoothoot begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.

species.name: Hoppip
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon drifts and floats with the wind. If it senses the approach of strong winds, a Hoppip links leaves with others to prepare against being blown away.

species.name: Horsea
species.pokedex_entry: By cleverly flicking the fins on its back side to side, it moves in any direction while facing forward. It spits ink to escape if it senses danger.

species.name: Houndoom
species.pokedex_entry: In a Houndoom pack, the one with its horns raked sharply back serves a leadership role. They choose their leader by fighting among themselves.

species.name: Houndour
species.pokedex_entry: Houndour communicate with each other using a variety of cries to corner their prey. This Pokémon's remarkable teamwork is simply unparalleled.

species.name: Houndstone
species.pokedex_entry: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

species.name: Huntail
species.pokedex_entry: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

species.name: Hydrapple
species.pokedex_entry: These capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.

species.name: Hydreigon
species.pokedex_entry: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

species.name: Hypno
species.pokedex_entry: The arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum in a Hypno's hand lull the foe into deep hypnosis. While searching for prey, it polishes the pendulum.

species.name: Igglybuff
species.pokedex_entry: Its soft and pliable body is very bouncy. When it sings continuously with all its might, its body steadily turns a deepening pink color.

species.name: Illumise
species.pokedex_entry: Illumise leads a flight of illuminated Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. This Pokémon is said to earn greater respect from its peers by composing more complex designs in the sky.

species.name: Impidimp
species.pokedex_entry: It sneaks into people’s homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.

species.name: Incineroar
species.pokedex_entry: Incineroar’s rough and aggressive behavior is its most notable trait, but the way it helps out small Pokémon shows that it has a kind side as well.

species.name: Indeedee
species.pokedex_entry: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

species.name: Infernape
species.pokedex_entry: It uses a special kind of martial arts involving all its limbs. Its fire never goes out.

species.name: Inkay
species.pokedex_entry: By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.

species.name: Inteleon
species.pokedex_entry: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes’ weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

species.name: Iron Boulder
species.pokedex_entry: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

species.name: Iron Bundle
species.pokedex_entry: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

species.name: Iron Crown
species.pokedex_entry: There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.

species.name: Iron Hands
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

species.name: Iron Jugulis
species.pokedex_entry: It’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

species.name: Iron Leaves
species.pokedex_entry: According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

species.name: Iron Moth
species.pokedex_entry: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.

species.name: Iron Thorns
species.pokedex_entry: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

species.name: Iron Treads
species.pokedex_entry: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

species.name: Iron Valiant
species.pokedex_entry: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

species.name: Ivysaur
species.pokedex_entry: To support its bulb, Ivysaur's legs grow sturdy. If it spends more time lying in the sunlight, the bud will soon bloom into a large flower.

species.name: Jangmo-o
species.pokedex_entry: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

species.name: Jellicent
species.pokedex_entry: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

species.name: Jigglypuff
species.pokedex_entry: Nothing can avoid falling asleep hearing a Jigglypuff's song. The sound waves of its singing voice match the brain waves of someone in a deep sleep.

species.name: Jirachi
species.pokedex_entry: Jirachi is said to make wishes come true. While it sleeps, a tough crystalline shell envelops the body to protect it from enemies.

species.name: Jolteon
species.pokedex_entry: Its cells generate weak power that is amplified by its fur's static electricity to drop thunderbolts. The bristling fur is made of electrically charged needles.

species.name: Joltik
species.pokedex_entry: They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

species.name: Jumpluff
species.pokedex_entry: Jumpluff ride warm southern winds to cross the sea and fly to foreign lands. This Pokémon lands when it encounters cold air while it is floating.

species.name: Jynx
species.pokedex_entry: A Jynx sashays rhythmically as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without noticing.

species.name: Kabuto
species.pokedex_entry: It is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in rare cases, living examples have been discovered. Kabuto have not changed for 300 million years.

species.name: Kabutops
species.pokedex_entry: Kabutops once swam underwater to hunt for prey. It was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from changes in its gills and legs.

species.name: Kadabra
species.pokedex_entry: It is rumored that a boy with psychic abilities suddenly transformed into Kadabra while he was assisting research into extrasensory powers.

species.name: Kakuna
species.pokedex_entry: It remains virtually immobile while it clings to a tree. However, on the inside, it busily prepares for evolution. This is evident from how hot its shell becomes.

species.name: Kangaskhan
species.pokedex_entry: If you come across a young Kangaskhan playing by itself, never try to catch it. The baby's parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged.

species.name: Karrablast
