species.pokedex_entry: The lightning-wielding guardian deity of Melemele, Tapu Koko is brimming with curiosity and appears before people from time to time.

species.name: Tapu Lele
species.pokedex_entry: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.

species.name: Tarountula
species.pokedex_entry: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

species.name: Tatsugiri
species.pokedex_entry: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

species.name: Tauros
species.pokedex_entry: It is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for Tauros to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.

species.name: Teddiursa
species.pokedex_entry: It licks its palms that are sweetened by being soaked in honey. A Teddiursa makes its own honey by blending fruits and pollen collected by Beedrill.

species.name: Tentacool
species.pokedex_entry: Its body is almost entirely composed of water. It ensnares its foe with its two long tentacles, then stabs with the poison stingers at their tips.

species.name: Tentacruel
species.pokedex_entry: It lives in complex rock formations on the ocean floor and traps prey using its 80 tentacles. Its red orbs glow when it grows excited or agitated.

species.name: Tepig
species.pokedex_entry: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.

species.name: Terapagos
species.pokedex_entry: It’s thought that this Pokémon lived in ancient Paldea until it got caught in seismic shifts and went extinct.

species.name: Terrakion
species.pokedex_entry: In Unovan legend, Terrakion battled against humans in an effort to protect other Pokémon.

species.name: Thievul
species.pokedex_entry: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

species.name: Throh
species.pokedex_entry: They train in groups of five. Any member that can’t keep up will discard its belt and leave the group.

species.name: Thundurus
species.pokedex_entry: They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil.

species.name: Thwackey
species.pokedex_entry: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.

species.name: Timburr
species.pokedex_entry: Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.

species.name: Ting-Lu
species.pokedex_entry: It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

species.name: Tinkatink
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it’s satisfied with the result.

species.name: Tinkaton
species.pokedex_entry: The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.

species.name: Tinkatuff
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other’s hammers by smashing them together.

species.name: Tirtouga
species.pokedex_entry: Tirtouga is considered to be the ancestor of many turtle Pokémon. It was restored to life from a fossil.

species.name: Toedscool
species.pokedex_entry: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

species.name: Toedscruel
species.pokedex_entry: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

species.name: Togedemaru
species.pokedex_entry: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

species.name: Togekiss
species.pokedex_entry: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

species.name: Togepi
species.pokedex_entry: As its energy, it uses the feelings of compassion and pleasure exuded by people and Pokémon. It stores up happy feelings in its shell, then shares them out.

species.name: Togetic
species.pokedex_entry: It is said to be a Pokémon that brings good fortune. When it spots someone who is pure of heart, a Togetic appears and shares its happiness with that person.

species.name: Torchic
species.pokedex_entry: If attacked, it strikes back by spitting balls of fire it forms in its stomach. A Torchic dislikes darkness because it can't see its surroundings.

species.name: Torkoal
species.pokedex_entry: It battles using energy it gets from burning coal. When loosing smoke from its nostrils, it lets off a sound that is similar to a locomotive's horn.

species.name: Tornadus
species.pokedex_entry: This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one.

species.name: Torracat
species.pokedex_entry: When facing a powerful enemy, Torracat’s fighting spirit gets pumped up, and its fire bell blazes hotter.

species.name: Torterra
species.pokedex_entry: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

species.name: Totodile
species.pokedex_entry: Despite its small body, Totodile's jaws are very powerful. While it may think it is just playfully nipping, its bite has enough strength to cause serious injury.

species.name: Toucannon
species.pokedex_entry: Known for forming harmonious couples, this Pokémon is brought to wedding ceremonies as a good luck charm.

species.name: Toxapex
species.pokedex_entry: Those attacked by Toxapex's poison will suffer intense pain for three days and three nights. Post-recovery, there will be some aftereffects.

species.name: Toxel
species.pokedex_entry: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

species.name: Toxicroak
species.pokedex_entry: Its crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.

species.name: Toxtricity
species.pokedex_entry: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

species.name: Tranquill
species.pokedex_entry: These bright Pokémon have acute memories. Apparently delivery workers often choose them as their partners.

species.name: Trapinch
species.pokedex_entry: Its big jaws crunch through boulders. Because its head is so big, it has a hard time getting back upright if it tips over onto its back.

species.name: Treecko
species.pokedex_entry: It makes its nest in a giant tree in the forest. It ferociously guards against anything nearing its territory. It is said to be the protector of the forest's trees.

species.name: Trevenant
species.pokedex_entry: Through its roots, it exerts control over other trees. A deadly curse falls upon anyone cutting down trees in forests where Trevenant dwell.

species.name: Tropius
species.pokedex_entry: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

species.name: Trubbish
species.pokedex_entry: The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.

species.name: Trumbeak
species.pokedex_entry: From its mouth, it fires the seeds of berries it has eaten. The scattered seeds give rise to new plants.

species.name: Tsareena
species.pokedex_entry: A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon’s legs leaves a wound in the opponent’s body and soul that will never heal.

species.name: Turtonator
species.pokedex_entry: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

species.name: Turtwig
species.pokedex_entry: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

species.name: Tympole
species.pokedex_entry: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.

species.name: Tynamo
species.pokedex_entry: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

species.name: Type: Null
species.pokedex_entry: It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.

species.name: Typhlosion
species.pokedex_entry: It can hide behind a shimmering heat haze that it creates using its intense flames. Typhlosion create blazing explosive blasts that burn everything to cinders.

species.name: Tyranitar
species.pokedex_entry: A Tyranitar is so overwhelmingly powerful, it can bring down a whole mountain to make its nest. It roams in mountains seeking new opponents to fight.

species.name: Tyrantrum
species.pokedex_entry: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

species.name: Tyrogue
species.pokedex_entry: Tyrogue become stressed out if they do not get to train every day. When raising this Pokémon, the trainer must establish a regular training schedule.

species.name: Tyrunt
species.pokedex_entry: Its immense jaws have enough destructive force that it can chew up an automobile. It lived 100 million years ago.

species.name: Umbreon
species.pokedex_entry: Umbreon evolved from exposure to the moon's energy pulses. It lurks in darkness and waits for its foes to move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.

species.name: Unfezant
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon is intelligent and intensely proud. People will sit up and take notice if you become the Trainer of one.

species.name: Unown
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon is shaped like ancient text characters. Although research is ongoing, it is a mystery as to which came first, the ancient writings or the various Unown.

species.name: Ursaluna
species.pokedex_entry: I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

species.name: Ursaring
species.pokedex_entry: In forests, it is said that there are many streams and towering trees where an Ursaring gathers food. It walks through its forest collecting food every day.

species.name: Urshifu
species.pokedex_entry: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.

species.name: Uxie
species.pokedex_entry: A Pokémon feared but also respected for stealing away the memories of evildoers. I have found records that suggest Uxie holds dominion over knowledge.

species.name: Vanillish
species.pokedex_entry: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.

species.name: Vanillite
species.pokedex_entry: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.

species.name: Vanilluxe
species.pokedex_entry: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Vaporeon
species.pokedex_entry: Vaporeon underwent a spontaneous mutation and grew fins and gills that allow them to live underwater. They have the ability to freely control water.

species.name: Varoom
species.pokedex_entry: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

species.name: Veluza
species.pokedex_entry: Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

species.name: Venipede
species.pokedex_entry: It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggresive.

species.name: Venomoth
species.pokedex_entry: Venomoth are nocturnal--they only are active at night. Their favorite prey are insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.

species.name: Venonat
species.pokedex_entry: Its coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body is said to have evolved for protection. Its large eyes never fail to spot even miniscule prey.

species.name: Venusaur
species.pokedex_entry: Venusaur's flower is said to take on vivid colors if it gets plenty of nutrition and sunlight. The flower's aroma soothes the emotions of people.

species.name: Vespiquen
species.pokedex_entry: Commands its subjects to build its hive. It will dispatch any interlopers who dare sneak into its nest and use them as nourishment for itself.

species.name: Vibrava
species.pokedex_entry: It looses ultrasonic waves by rubbing its wings together. Since a Vibrava's wings are still in the process of growing, it can only fly short distances.

species.name: Victini
species.pokedex_entry: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

species.name: Victreebel
species.pokedex_entry: The long vine extending from its head is waved about as if it were a living thing to attract prey. When an unsuspecting victim approaches, it is swallowed whole.

species.name: Vigoroth
species.pokedex_entry: It can't keep still because its blood boils with energy. It runs through the fields and mountains all day to calm itself. If it doesn't, it can't sleep at night.

species.name: Vikavolt
species.pokedex_entry: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.

species.name: Vileplume
species.pokedex_entry: In seasons when it produces more pollen, the air around a Vileplume turns yellow with the powder as it walks. The pollen is highly toxic and causes paralysis.

species.name: Virizion
species.pokedex_entry: It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns.

species.name: Vivillon
species.pokedex_entry: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

species.name: Volbeat
species.pokedex_entry: Volbeat’s tail glows like a lightbulb. With other Volbeat, it uses its tail to draw geometric shapes in the night sky. This Pokémon loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

species.name: Volcanion
species.pokedex_entry: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

species.name: Volcarona
species.pokedex_entry: A sea of fire engulfs the surroundings of their battles, since they use their six wings to scatter their ember scales.

species.name: Voltorb
species.pokedex_entry: It bears an uncanny and unexplained resemblance to a Pok? Ball. Because it explodes at the slightest shock, even veteran trainers treat it with caution.

species.name: Vullaby
species.pokedex_entry: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.

species.name: Vulpix
species.pokedex_entry: It can freely control fire, making fiery orbs fly like will-o'-the-wisps. Just before evolution, its six tails grow hot as if on fire.

species.name: Wailmer
species.pokedex_entry: While this Pokémon usually lives in the sea, it can survive on land, although not too long. It loses vitality if its body becomes dried out.

species.name: Wailord
species.pokedex_entry: It breathes through nostrils that it raises above the sea. By inhaling to its maximum capacity, a Wailord can dive close to 10,000 feet beneath the waves.

species.name: Walking Wake
species.pokedex_entry: It resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.

species.name: Walrein
species.pokedex_entry: Its thick tusks are strong enough to shatter drift ice. They have been known to break, but they will grow back by the next year. The Hisui region is well known for these broken tusks.

species.name: Wartortle
species.pokedex_entry: Its large tail is covered with rich, thick fur that deepens in color with age. The scratches on its shell are evidence of this Pokémon's toughness in battle.

species.name: Watchog
species.pokedex_entry: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

species.name: Wattrel
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.

species.name: Weavile
species.pokedex_entry: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

species.name: Weedle
species.pokedex_entry: A Weedle has an extremely acute sense of smell. It distinguishes its favorite kinds of leaves from those it dislikes by sniffing with its big red proboscis (nose).

species.name: Weepinbell
species.pokedex_entry: At night, a Weepinbell hangs on to a tree branch with its hooked rear and sleeps. If it moves around in its sleep, it may wake up to find itself on the ground.

species.name: Weezing
species.pokedex_entry: By diluting its toxic gases with a special process, the highest grade of perfume can be extracted. To Weezing, gases emanating from garbage are the ultimate feast.

species.name: Whimsicott
species.pokedex_entry: Riding whirlwinds, they appear. These Pokémon sneak through gaps into houses and cause all sorts of mischief.

species.name: Whirlipede
species.pokedex_entry: It is usually motionless, but when attacked, it rotates at high speed and then crashes into its opponent.

species.name: Whiscash
species.pokedex_entry: Strikes its caudal fin against the swamp bed to shake the ground and startle its prey. It will then swallow the fleeing prey whole. People mistook this behavior as the cause of earthquakes.

species.name: Whismur
species.pokedex_entry: When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder. It cries until it’s exhausted, then it falls asleep.

species.name: Wigglytuff
species.pokedex_entry: Its fur is the ultimate in luxuriousness. Sleeping alongside a Wigglytuff is simply divine. Its body expands seemingly without end when it inhales.

species.name: Wiglett
species.pokedex_entry: Though it looks like Diglett, Wiglett is an entirely different species. The resemblance seems to be a coincidental result of environmental adaptation.

species.name: Wimpod
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

species.name: Wingull
species.pokedex_entry: It soars on updrafts without flapping its wings. It makes a nest on sheer cliffs at the sea’s edge.

species.name: Wishiwashi
species.pokedex_entry: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

species.name: Wo-Chien
species.pokedex_entry: It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

species.name: Wobbuffet
species.pokedex_entry: Usually docile, a Wobbuffet strikes back ferociously if its black tail is attacked. It makes its lair in caves where it waits for nightfall.

species.name: Woobat
species.pokedex_entry: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

species.name: Wooloo
species.pokedex_entry: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

species.name: Wooper
species.pokedex_entry: Wooper usually live in water but come out onto land seeking food occasionally. On land, they coat their bodies with a gooey, toxic film.

species.name: Wormadam
species.pokedex_entry: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

species.name: Wugtrio
species.pokedex_entry: A variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.

species.name: Wurmple
species.pokedex_entry: Likes sap and is abundant in the wild. Why it evolves into various different forms is unknown. One cannot tell from a Wurmple's appearance which form it will take when it evolves.

species.name: Wynaut
species.pokedex_entry: A Wynaut loves to eat sweet fruits. It cleverly picks fruits using its earlike arms. They gather in fruit gardens, drawn by the fragrance.

species.name: Wyrdeer
species.pokedex_entry: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

species.name: Xatu
species.pokedex_entry: It has the enigmatic power of foreseeing the future. Some people in different lands have long believed that Xatu are emissaries from another world.

species.name: Xerneas
species.pokedex_entry: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

species.name: Xurkitree
species.pokedex_entry: They’ve been dubbed Ultra Beasts. Some of them stand unmoving, like trees, with their arms and legs stuck into the ground.

species.name: Yamask
species.pokedex_entry: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves in past ages. Each retains memories of its former life.

species.name: Yamper
species.pokedex_entry: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

species.name: Yanma
species.pokedex_entry: It can see 360 degrees without moving its eyes. It is a great flier capable of making sudden stops and turning midair to quickly chase down targeted prey.

species.name: Yanmega
species.pokedex_entry: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

species.name: Yungoos
species.pokedex_entry: Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.

species.name: Yveltal
species.pokedex_entry: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

species.name: Zacian
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon has slumbered for many years. Some say it’s Zamazenta’s elder sister—others say the two Pokémon are rivals.

species.name: Zamazenta
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon slept for aeons while in the form of a statue. It was asleep for so long, people forgot that it ever existed.

species.name: Zangoose
species.pokedex_entry: Zangoose usually stays on all fours, but when angered, it gets up on its hind legs and extends its claws. This Pokémon shares a bitter rivalry with Seviper that dates back over generations.

species.name: Zapdos
species.pokedex_entry: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon that has the ability to control electricity. It usually lives in thunderclouds. It gains power if it is stricken by lightning bolts.

species.name: Zarude
species.pokedex_entry: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

species.name: Zebstrika
species.pokedex_entry: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

species.name: Zekrom
species.pokedex_entry: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

species.name: Zeraora
species.pokedex_entry: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

species.name: Zigzagoon
species.pokedex_entry: Zigzagoon that adapted to regions outside Galar acquired this appearance. If you’ve lost something, this Pokémon can likely find it.

species.name: Zoroark
species.pokedex_entry: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

species.name: Zorua
species.pokedex_entry: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

species.name: Zubat
species.pokedex_entry: While living in pitch-black caverns, their eyes gradually grew shut and deprived them of vision. They use ultrasonic waves to detect obstacles.

species.name: Zweilous
species.pokedex_entry: Since their two heads do not get along and compete with each other for food, they always eat too much.

species.name: Zygarde
species.pokedex_entry: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.
